paper_metadata.title: Big Bird: Transformers for Longer Sequences
paper_metadata.abstract: Transformers-based models, such as BERT, have been one of the most successful deep learning models for NLP. Unfortunately, one of their core limitations is the quadratic dependency (mainly in terms of memory) on the sequence length due to their full attention mechanism. To remedy this, we propose, BigBird, a sparse attention mechanism that reduces this quadratic dependency to linear. We show that BigBird is a universal approximator of sequence functions and is Turing complete, thereby preserving these properties of the quadratic, full attention model. Along the way, our theoretical analysis reveals some of the benefits of having $O(1)$ global tokens (such as CLS), that attend to the entire sequence as part of the sparse attention mechanism. The proposed sparse attention can handle sequences of length up to 8x of what was previously possible using similar hardware. As a consequence of the capability to handle longer context, BigBird drastically improves performance on various NLP tasks such as question answering and summarization. We also propose novel applications to genomics data.

extraction_data.innovation: BIGBIRD introduces a sparse attention mechanism with global tokens, enabling linear scaling with sequence length and preserving model expressiveness for long sequences.
extraction_data.application: Natural language processing (question answering, long document classification), genomics (promoter region prediction, chromatin-profile prediction)
extraction_data.research_problem: Long sequence processing for natural language understanding and genomics
extraction_data.extension: Uses global tokens to preserve model expressiveness in sparse attention.
extraction_data.paper_title: Big Bird: Transformers for Longer Sequences

extraction_data.innovation: BIGBIRD's primary innovation is the use of global-local attention with relative position encodings, enabling better handling of structured inputs. Unlike Longformer, BIGBIRD trains global tokens using CPC loss and learns their usage during fine-tuning.
extraction_data.application: Question Answering (TriviaQA, HotpotQA, WikiHop), Document Classification (IMDb, Yelp-5, Arxiv, Patents, Hyperpartisan), GLUE tasks
extraction_data.extension: Global-local attention with relative position encodings and CPC loss for training global tokens.
extraction_data.paper_title: Big Bird: Transformers for Longer Sequences

extraction_data.innovation: BIGBIRD's primary innovation is the use of global-local attention with relative position encodings, enabling better handling of structured inputs. Unlike Longformer, BIGBIRD trains global tokens using CPC loss and learns their usage during fine-tuning.
extraction_data.application: Question Answering (TriviaQA, HotpotQA, WikiHop), Document Classification (IMDb, Yelp-5, Arxiv, Patents, Hyperpartisan), GLUE tasks
extraction_data.extension: Global-local attention with relative position encodings and CPC loss for training global tokens.
extraction_data.paper_title: Big Bird: Transformers for Longer Sequences

extraction_data.innovation: BIGBIRD's primary innovation is a sparse attention mechanism that enables processing of longer sequences with linear complexity, as opposed to the quadratic complexity of standard attention.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Human reference GRCh37 (genomic), BooksCorpus, English Wikipedia (NLP)
extraction_data.pretraining_task: Masked Language Model (MLM), Next Sentence Prediction (NSP)
extraction_data.paper_title: Big Bird: Transformers for Longer Sequences
